sections.content:

You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 43, level 1.
Valid actions right now: MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: After clicking the dark gray diamond at [55, 28], the two diamonds swapped colors! Node 16 (was white, now dark gray) and Node 21 (was dark gray, now white). The white diamond is now at the bottom position [54-56, 26-30], and the dark gray diamond is at [47-49, 28-31]. The magenta pixel at [51, 28] is still there. Now the white diamond is closer to the magenta pixel from below.
- Goal model: The goal is likely to grow the chain by clicking the magenta pixel, then click the circle to pull the diamond, repeating until the level completes.
- Plan: Try clicking the magenta pixel at [51, 28] again now that the white diamond has moved to the bottom.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 42 actions; a strong score needs about 22 or fewer. You are 1.9x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the current state after clicking [51, 28]:
- Node 16: color=W (white diamond) - still white at [47-49, 28-31]
- Node 21: color=G (dark gray diamond) - still dark gray at [54-56, 26-30]
- Node 19: color=M (magenta pixel) - still at [51, 28]
- Node 15: color=W, pixels=19 (timer bar shrank from 20 to 19)
Wait, clicking [51, 28] again didn't change anything except the timer! The magenta pixel is still there. The diamonds didn't swap back.
Let me reconsider the game mechanics. Looking at the history:
1. The white diamond moves down by clicking magenta pixels
2. When the white diamond reaches the bottom (dark gray diamond position), clicking that diamond swaps colors
3. But clicking the magenta pixel again doesn't do anything now
Maybe the game flow is:
- Click magenta pixels to move the white diamond down the chain
- When white diamond reaches the bottom, click the dark gray diamond to swap
- Now the white diamond is at the bottom, and the dark gray is at the top
- The magenta pixel at [51, 28] is now between the two diamonds
- Maybe I need to click the circle to "reset" or "pull" the diamond back up?
Or maybe the magenta pixel at [51, 28] is a different kind of obstacle that needs a different approach.
Let me look at the purple chain (Node 18) more carefully. It spans from row 49 to row 54. The magenta pixel at [51, 28] is embedded within this chain.
Wait - maybe the game is about connecting the white diamond to the circle at the top. The white diamond needs to travel up through the purple chain to reach the circle. 
Currently the white diamond is at the top (Node 16: [47-49, 28-31]) and the dark gray is at the bottom (Node 21: [54-56, 26-30]). The magenta pixel is at [51, 28].
Hmm, but the white diamond is already at the top. Maybe I need to click the circle to complete the level?
Let me try clicking the circle (purple ring). The circle is made of nodes 3-14, all color=p. The center of the circle is around [21, 39].

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Clicking [51, 28] again had no effect - only the timer bar shrank (20→19). The magenta pixel persists, diamonds stayed in their swapped positions. The white diamond is at [47-49, 28-31] (top), dark gray at [54-56, 26-30] (bottom), magenta at [51, 28] (middle). Since clicking the magenta pixel twice didn't work, maybe I need to click the purple circle at the top to complete the level or trigger the next phase.
Plan: Click the center of the purple circle to test if that advances the level.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click the center of the purple circle
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 39}])
print("Action result:", result)

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print(f"\nStep: {current_frame.step}")
for node in nodes:
    c = node['color']
    p = node['pixels']
    if c in ['W', 'G', 'M', 'p'] and p <= 25:
        print(f"Node {node['id']}: color={c}, pixels={p}, boundary={node['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
